_meta.description: Three-layer payer identification and routing engine. Layer 1: name matching. Layer 2: ID pattern / card clue detection. Layer 3: unknown payer protocol with Carelon baseline fallback. Feeds into payer_*.json files for operational rules and criteria_*.json for clinical standards.
_meta.relationship_to_payer_criteria_map: This file is the FRONT DOOR that was always missing from payer_criteria_map.json. It resolves OCR-extracted text into a payer_id, which then loads the corresponding payer JSON (individual files) or payer_criteria_map entry (legacy). The criteria map assumed the payer was already identified -- this file does the identifying.

resolution_order.description: Try these layers in order. Stop at first confident match.
resolution_order.layer_1: Name matching -- compare OCR-extracted payer name against known names
resolution_order.layer_2: ID pattern detection -- analyze member ID format, card logos, network indicators
resolution_order.layer_3: Unknown protocol -- process clinicals against Carelon baseline, flag for verification

layer_1_name_routes._usage: Normalize OCR text to lowercase. Try exact match, then substring match, then fuzzy match. Multiple names map to same payer_id.

aetna medicare.notes: Aetna Medicare still routes through Evicore

anthem bcbs medicaid.notes: Uses Carelon criteria but submits via Availity/fax -- NOT Carelon portal

electrical workers.notes: BCBS union plan. Prefixes QEW, AFJ.

operating engineers.notes: BCBS union plan. Prefix OEHOE. NO AUTH per prefix DB.

southwest carpenters.notes: BCBS union plan. Prefixes N5X, EFWSC.

teamsters.notes: BCBS union plan. Prefixes JTL, JTFZBL, TDGZHC.

great west health care.notes: Acquired by Cigna. Verify if processing through Evicore.

united healthcare.notes: Default to Commercial -- check for Medicare/Optum during benefits

surest.notes: Variable copay -- must call for accurate copay

humana.notes: Check for Optum IPA during benefits

tricare.notes: Determine Prime vs Select during verification

medicare.notes: Default FFS. If Medicare Advantage, reroute to parent company.

medicare advantage.notes: Identify parent company and reroute to that payer_id

umr.notes: Check portal redirect for Sierra vs UHC network

pebp.notes: Public Employees Benefits Program. HPN portal.

hometown health.notes: Local NV plan. HPN portal. HST 95806.

teachers health trust.notes: NO AUTH any code. HST 80/20. In-lab $75 copay.

dcmwc.notes: Division of Coal Mine Workers Compensation

ghi (emblem health).notes: NY plan. OON for NV. Multiplan when applicable.

medi-share.notes: Health sharing ministry. In-lab only. No HST rate.

layer_2_pattern_detection._usage: When Layer 1 name match fails or returns ambiguous result, analyze the member ID format, card features, and network indicators to narrow identification.

id_format_patterns._description: Regex patterns against the member ID string. Test in order -- first match wins.

bcbs_alpha_prefix.description: 3-letter prefix = BCBS plan. Extract prefix, look up in bcbs_prefix_database.json.

all_numeric_after_prefix.signal: Typically NOT Carelon. Chat verification usually unavailable. Call or use payer portal.

alpha_numeric_mixed.signal: Usually Carelon or No Auth. Chat verification typically works.

extended_numeric.signal: Unusual. Special plan. Call to verify.

bcbs_federal_r_prefix.description: Single R + 8+ digits = BCBS Federal Employee Program

medicare_mbi.description: Medicare Beneficiary Identifier format: 1C4D5E6F7G89 pattern (digit-letter alternating, 11 chars)
medicare_mbi.notes: If patient also has a commercial plan, this may be secondary. Check COB.

medicaid_nv.description: 10-12 digit numeric ID with no letters = likely Nevada Medicaid
medicaid_nv.notes: Could also be MCO Medicaid -- need to determine which MCO. Use medicaid_mco_routing.

tricare_dod.notes: Could also be SSN-based. Confirm with card or patient.

uhc_format.notes: Many payers use 9-digit IDs. Only use as tiebreaker with other clues.

card_clue_detection._description: OCR may extract logos, network names, phone numbers, or other text from insurance cards. These clues help identify the payer even when the name is unclear.

phone_number_clues._description: Certain phone numbers on cards are associated with specific networks. OCR may extract these.

800-810-2583.notes: Evicore main line -- likely Cigna or Aetna plan

network_text_indicators._description: Text found on card that indicates network affiliation

evicore.notes: Evicore on card = Cigna or Aetna. Check payer name.

optum.notes: Optum on card = likely UHC Medicare Optum-managed

availity.notes: Many payers use Availity -- not a payer identifier by itself

innovative care.notes: Innovative Care Mgmt = BCBS union plan auth handler

nevada health solutions.notes: NV Health Solutions = BCBS union plan auth handler

self_funded_employer_detection._description: When card shows an employer name instead of an insurance company, try to identify the TPA or network
clues: If card shows employer name + BCBS logo -> BCBS plan (use prefix routing)
If card shows employer name + UHC logo -> UHC plan (check uhcprovider.com)
If card shows employer name + Multiplan/PHCS/First Health -> rental network (call IBA)
If card shows employer name + EBMS -> TPA (call EBMS number)
If card shows employer name + 'Administered by [name]' -> that name is the TPA
If card shows employer name + no other identifiers -> call number on card to determine handler

layer_3_unknown_protocol._description: When Layer 1 and Layer 2 both fail to identify the payer. Does NOT block processing. Uses Carelon baseline for clinical sufficiency check while flagging for verification.
workflow: 1. Process document normally -- extract all clinical data
2. Check clinical sufficiency against Carelon baseline criteria
6. If still unknown: staff calls number on card
7. During call, gather: payer name, network, auth required per code, HST code, submission method, benefits
8. After call: combine auth status with clinical readiness for final output
9. Fire FLAG_ADD_TO_SYSTEM to catalog new payer for future

conditional_output.meets_baseline: If auth required, documentation likely meets criteria
conditional_output.does_not_meet_baseline: If auth required, additional documentation likely needed

FLAG_UNKNOWN_PAYER.action: Call number on card. Determine auth requirements, HST code, submission method.

FLAG_CLINICAL_BASELINE_MET.action: Note: if auth required, documentation likely meets criteria

FLAG_CLINICAL_BASELINE_NOT_MET.action: Note: if auth required, additional documentation likely needed

FLAG_ADD_TO_SYSTEM.action: Add newly identified payer to insurance.json and create payer routing entry

escalation: If unable to identify payer after call: escalate to supervisor
If patient has no card: request card, cannot proceed without verifiable info
If truly unresolvable: schedule as self-pay pending verification

secondary_routing._description: These are secondary routing steps that run AFTER initial payer_id is determined, to refine the routing.

bcbs_prefix_routing.process: Extract prefix from member ID -> look up in bcbs_prefix_database.json -> refine to Carelon vs non-Carelon, get specific auth method per prefix
bcbs_prefix_routing.union_note: BCBS union plans have specific prefixes with custom auth routing per prefix

medicare_advantage_routing.triggers_when: payer_id == 'medicare_ffs' AND plan appears to be Medicare Advantage (not original Medicare)
medicare_advantage_routing.process: Identify parent company -> reroute to parent's payer_id

medicaid_mco_routing.triggers_when: payer_id == 'nevada_medicaid_ffs' AND patient has MCO (not straight FFS)
medicaid_mco_routing.process: Determine which MCO -> reroute to MCO's payer_id

uhc_plan_type_routing.process: During benefits verification, determine: Commercial vs Medicare Direct vs Medicare Optum vs Surest. Refine payer_id accordingly.

humana_plan_type_routing.process: During benefits check via Availity, check for Optum IPA or P3/Intermountain IPA.

refinement.optum_ipa_found: Use Optum criteria, submit via Curo portal (NOT Humana criteria)

do_not_accept.action: STOP_OON -- fax UTS to referring provider
